title: Implement edge caching for public pages with Cache-Control headers
description:
## Summary
Add Cache-Control headers to public pages (explore, content detail, org landing) to enable Cloudflare edge caching.

## Pages to Cache

| Route | Cache-Control | Notes |
|-------|--------------|-------|
| `/[org]/explore` | `public, max-age=300, s-maxage=300` | 5 min |
| `/[org]/content/[slug]` | `public, max-age=300, s-maxage=300` | 5 min |
| `/[org]` (landing) | `public, max-age=300, s-maxage=300` | 5 min |
| `/` (platform home) | `public, max-age=3600, s-maxage=3600` | 1 hour |
| `/creators/[username]` | `public, max-age=300, s-maxage=300` | 5 min |

## Pages NOT to Cache

| Route | Cache-Control | Reason |
|-------|--------------|--------|
| `/library` | `private, no-cache` | User-specific |
| `/studio/*` | `private, no-cache` | Authenticated |
| `/account/*` | `private, no-cache` | User-specific |

## Implementation
In SvelteKit, set headers in load function:
```typescript
export async function load({ setHeaders }) {
  setHeaders({
    'Cache-Control': 'public, max-age=300, s-maxage=300'
  });
  // ... fetch data
}
```

## Client-Side Hydration
Cached pages serve static shell. User-specific state loads client-side:
- Purchase status (show Buy vs Play button)
- Playback progress
- Wishlist state

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Public pages return correct Cache-Control headers
- [ ] Authenticated pages return private/no-cache
- [ ] Verify caching with `curl -I` and CF-Cache-Status header
- [ ] Client-side hydration works for user state
close_reason: Implemented by agent team: layout-builder, video-builder, infra-builder

title: Identity-API: Add organization membership lookup endpoint
description:
Add endpoint to check user's role in an organization. Needed for SvelteKit hooks to resolve org membership.

**Endpoint:**
```
GET /api/organizations/:orgId/membership/:userId
```

**Response:**
```json
{
  "data": {
    "role": "owner" | "admin" | "creator" | "subscriber" | "member" | null,
    "joinedAt": "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z"
  }
}
```

**Implementation:**
- Query `organizationMemberships` table
- Return null role if no membership exists
- No auth required (SvelteKit server calls this internally)

**Validation:**
- orgId: UUID
- userId: UUID
close_reason: Membership endpoint implemented: GET /api/organizations/:orgId/membership/:userId. Types, route, tests all passing.

Build a full content catalog page for a creator with filtering and pagination.

2-3 hours

Depends on CREATOR-01 (Codex-tu8)

title: ACCT-03: Account Payment Settings
Configure R2 CDN caching for thumbnails and static assets
description:
## Context
Thumbnails served from R2 via Cloudflare CDN. Need optimal cache headers for cost/performance.

## Requirements

### Cache Headers for Thumbnails
```
Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable
```
- Thumbnails are content-addressed (contentId in URL)
- Never change once generated
- 1 year cache is safe

### Cache Headers for HLS Segments
```
Cache-Control: public, max-age=86400
```
- Video segments can be cached 24h
- Manifest files shorter (1h) if live updates needed

### R2 Configuration
- Set default cache headers on R2 bucket
- OR set via Cloudflare Transform Rules

## Cost Impact
- Proper caching reduces R2 egress (Class B operations)
- Edge cache hits = no origin requests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Configure cache headers on R2 bucket for /thumbnails/*
- [ ] Configure cache headers for /media/* (HLS content)
- [ ] Verify headers with curl -I
- [ ] Document cache invalidation strategy if thumbnails ever need refresh
close_reason: Cache-Control headers set on all R2 uploads via image-processing service (immutable 1-year cache for content-addressed paths). HLS caching deferred to when HLS pipeline is fully wired.

title: Remove mediaBucket from IdentityServiceConfig
description:
Service registry references mediaBucket property that doesn't exist in IdentityServiceConfig.

Impact: TypeScript compilation error (how did this pass CI?).

Location: packages/worker-utils/src/procedure/service-registry.ts:363-384

Fix: Remove mediaBucket param from IdentityService construction:
```typescript
_identity = new IdentityService({
  db: getSharedDb(),
  environment: getEnvironment(),
  r2Service,
  r2PublicUrlBase: env.R2_BUCKET_MEDIA,  // Use for URL base, not mediaBucket
});
```

Note: IdentityServiceConfig interface only has r2Service and r2PublicUrlBase (packages/identity/src/services/identity-service.ts:11-14)
close_reason: Closed

title: STUDIO-01: Studio Layout Shell
description:
## Objective
Create the Studio layout shell with sidebar navigation, header bar, and role-based gating. This is the foundational layout that all Studio/Admin pages render within.

## Files
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/+layout.svelte` — Layout component with sidebar + header + slot
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/+layout.server.ts` — Server load: fetch member role, gate access

## Implementation Approach
1. **Server load** (`+layout.server.ts`):
   - Call `api.org.getMemberContext(slug)` to fetch the authenticated user's role within the org (owner / admin / creator).
   - If the user is not at least a `creator` role, redirect to the org home page with a 403-style toast message.
   - Return `{ role, org, user }` to the layout.

2. **Layout component** (`+layout.svelte`):
   - Two-column layout: sidebar (left, 240px) + main content area (right, flex-1).
   - Header bar at top of main area with org name + user avatar dropdown.
   - Sidebar navigation items:
     - **Dashboard** — `/studio` (all roles)
     - **Content** — `/studio/content` (all roles)
     - **Media** — `/studio/media` (all roles)
     - **Analytics** — `/studio/analytics` (admin+ only)
     - **Customers** — `/studio/customers` (admin+ only)
     - **Team** — `/studio/team` (admin+ only)
     - **Settings** — `/studio/settings` (admin+ only)
     - **Billing** — `/studio/billing` (owner only)
   - Conditionally render nav items based on `data.role`.
   - Active item highlighted based on current `$page.url.pathname`.
   - Use existing `StudioSidebar` component if available, or create one.

3. **Responsive behavior**:
   - On viewports < 768px, sidebar collapses into a hamburger menu / drawer overlay.
   - Use a `sidebarOpen` state variable toggled by a menu button in the header.

4. **Design tokens**: Use existing CSS custom properties for colors, spacing, typography.

## API Endpoints
- `GET /api/org/:slug/member-context` → returns `{ role, org, user }`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Server load fetches member role and gates access (non-creators redirected)
- [ ] Sidebar renders all nav items with correct role-based visibility
- [ ] Active nav item is visually highlighted
- [ ] Responsive: sidebar collapses to drawer on mobile (<768px)
- [ ] Layout provides `<slot>` for child routes
- [ ] TypeScript types for load data are correct
- [ ] No layout shift on initial load

## Effort Estimate
4–5 hours

## Dependencies
- ORG-03 (org member context API — may not exist yet as a bead)
- Codex-tvf.4 (P1.4: Admin Control Implementation)
notes:
## Review Notes (Feb 2026)

### Code References to Use
- **StudioSidebar**: `StudioSidebar.svelte` already exists — wire it into the layout
- **StudioSwitcher**: `StudioSwitcher.svelte` already exists — for org switching in sidebar
- **Studio layout**: `_org/[slug]/studio/+layout.server.ts` exists (skeletal) — enhance it

### BACKEND BLOCKER
- **Codex-byh0 (BE-03)**: No user-facing org role check endpoint. Membership endpoint is HMAC-only.
- Need role info to show/hide admin features in sidebar
- **Workaround**: Can build layout shell now. Use session user data for basic auth check. Role-based feature gating waits for BE-03.

title: ECOM-01: Purchase Components (PurchaseButton, PurchaseCTA, PriceDisplay)
description:
## Objective
Create 3 reusable commerce UI components: PurchaseButton (buy action with loading/error states), PurchaseCTA (composite component for content purchase prompts), and PriceDisplay (formatted price with currency and size variants).

## Files
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/commerce/PurchaseButton.svelte` — Buy button with states
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/commerce/PurchaseCTA.svelte` — Composite purchase call-to-action
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/commerce/PriceDisplay.svelte` — Formatted price display
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/commerce/index.ts` — Barrel export

## Implementation Approach
1. **PriceDisplay component**:
   - Props: `amount: number`, `currency: string` (default "USD"), `size: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg'` (default "md").
   - Uses `Intl.NumberFormat` for locale-aware currency formatting.
   - Size variants:
     - `sm`: 14px, inline use
     - `md`: 18px, card/list use
     - `lg`: 24px, hero/detail use
   - Free content: renders as "$0.00" (or parent can choose to hide).

2. **PurchaseButton component**:
   - Props: `price: number`, `currency: string`, `contentId: string`, `disabled: boolean`, `onclick: () => Promise<void>`.
   - States:
     - **Default**: "Buy for $X.XX" with PriceDisplay inline.
     - **Loading**: "Redirecting to checkout..." with spinner, disabled.
     - **Error**: Red text error message below button, button re-enabled.
   - Button styling: primary action button with design tokens.
   - Calls `onclick` prop when clicked; parent handles checkout logic.

3. **PurchaseCTA component**:
   - Props: `price: number`, `currency: string`, `contentId: string`, `isFree: boolean`.
   - Composite layout:
     - **Free content**: Green "Free" badge (pill shape, green background, white text). No purchase button — show "Watch Now" or "Access" button instead.
     - **Paid content**: PriceDisplay (lg size) + PurchaseButton + guarantee text ("30-day money-back guarantee" or similar).
   - Vertically stacked layout with appropriate spacing.

4. **Barrel export** (`index.ts`): Export all 3 components for clean imports.

## API Endpoints
None — these are pure UI components. Checkout logic is wired in ECOM-02.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] PriceDisplay formats currency correctly using Intl.NumberFormat
- [ ] PriceDisplay renders in 3 size variants (sm, md, lg)
- [ ] PurchaseButton shows "Buy for $X.XX" in default state
- [ ] PurchaseButton shows spinner + "Redirecting to checkout..." in loading state
- [ ] PurchaseButton displays error message when purchase fails
- [ ] PurchaseCTA shows green "Free" badge for free content
- [ ] PurchaseCTA shows price + buy button + guarantee text for paid content
- [ ] All components use design tokens for consistent styling
- [ ] Components are typed with TypeScript Props interfaces
- [ ] Barrel export works correctly

## Effort Estimate
3–4 hours

## Dependencies
- CONTENT-03 (Content detail page — may not exist yet as a bead; these components are used on content pages)
notes:
## Review Notes (Feb 2026)

### Code References to Use
- **Badge**: Use existing `Badge` component from UI library for free badge
- **Spinner**: Use existing `Spinner` component for loading states
- **Price formatting**: Use `Intl.NumberFormat('en-US', { style: 'currency', currency: 'USD' })`
- **Checkout integration**: Wire PurchaseButton to `createCheckout` form or `createCheckoutSession` command from `lib/remote/checkout.remote.ts`

### No Backend Blockers
Pure UI component task. Fully unblocked.

title: WP-7: Platform Routes (Landing, Discover, Account)
description:
## Objective
Implement the platform-level routes that users access on the root domain.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Landing page (`/`) with marketing content
- [ ] Discover page (`/discover`) with org/creator search
- [ ] About page (`/about`)
- [ ] Pricing page (`/pricing`) - can be placeholder
- [ ] Account page (`/account`) with profile settings
- [ ] Account payment page (`/account/payment`)
- [ ] Account notifications page (`/account/notifications`)
- [ ] All pages have proper SEO meta tags
- [ ] Authenticated routes redirect to login if not authenticated

## Routes Structure
```
src/routes/(platform)/
├── +page.svelte               # Landing
├── +layout.svelte             # Platform layout
├── +layout.server.ts          # Load user for nav
├── about/
│   └── +page.svelte
├── pricing/
│   └── +page.svelte
├── discover/
│   ├── +page.svelte
│   └── +page.server.ts        # Load orgs/creators
└── account/
    ├── +layout.svelte         # Auth guard
    ├── +layout.server.ts      # Require auth
    ├── +page.svelte           # Profile settings
    ├── payment/
    │   └── +page.svelte
    └── notifications/
        └── +page.svelte
```

## Landing Page Sections
- Hero: Value proposition for creators
- Features: What Codex offers
- How it works: Simple steps
- CTA: Create your space / Sign up

## Discover Page
- Search bar (org name, creator name)
- Featured orgs grid
- Featured creators grid
- Categories (future)

## Account Pages
- Profile: Name, email, avatar
- Payment: Saved cards, billing history (read from Stripe)
- Notifications: Email preferences

## Auth Guard Pattern
```typescript
// +layout.server.ts
import { redirect } from '@sveltejs/kit';

export async function load({ locals }) {
  if (!locals.user) {
    throw redirect(303, '/login?redirect=/account');
  }
  return { user: locals.user };
}
```

## SEO Pattern
```typescript
// +page.server.ts
export async function load() {
  return {
    seo: {
      title: 'Discover Creators & Organizations | Codex',
      description: 'Find amazing content creators and organizations on Codex.',
    }
  };
}
```

## Effort
Large (L) - ~10-16 hours
close_reason: Closed

title: ERR-01: Custom Error Pages (404, 500)
description:
## Objective
Create/enhance the SvelteKit error page with custom designs for 404 (Not Found) and 500 (Server Error) states. Branded styling with design tokens, helpful navigation, and SEO noindex.

## Files
- apps/web/src/routes/+error.svelte - Main error page component (create or enhance)
- apps/web/src/lib/components/error/Error404.svelte - 404-specific content
- apps/web/src/lib/components/error/Error500.svelte - 500-specific content

## Implementation Approach
1. Error page (+error.svelte): Access SvelteKit page.error and page.status stores. Conditionally render Error404 or Error500 based on status code. Fallback for other status codes: generic error display. Centered layout, max-width 600px.

2. Error404 component: Large 404 display text. Page not found heading. The page you are looking for does not exist or has been moved description. Go Home primary button to /. Go Back text link via history.back().

3. Error500 component: Warning icon. Something went wrong heading. We are working on fixing this description. Try Again button via window.location.reload(). Go Home secondary button to /.

4. SEO: svelte:head with meta name robots content noindex on all error pages. Appropriate title tags.

5. Styling: Use design tokens (CSS custom properties). Responsive mobile and desktop. Dark mode support if tokens include dark variants.

6. ErrorBoundary pattern: Integrate with existing ErrorBoundary if available, otherwise use SvelteKit built-in +error.svelte.

## API Endpoints
None - error pages are client-side rendered by SvelteKit.

## Acceptance Criteria
- 404 page shows Page not found with home link and back navigation
- 500 page shows Something went wrong with retry button
- Both pages use branded styling with design tokens
- SEO noindex meta tag on all error pages
- Appropriate page titles set via svelte:head
- Responsive layout (mobile + desktop)
- Fallback rendering for other HTTP status codes
- Buttons have proper focus states and accessibility

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Dependencies
None - this is a standalone cross-cutting concern.

title: UI-02: Shared Pagination Component
description:
## Objective
Create a reusable Pagination component used across multiple pages: STUDIO-04 (Content), STUDIO-06 (Customers), LIB-03 (Library), CREATOR-02 (Creator Catalog), ORG-02 (Explore), ACCT-03 (Payment History). At least 6 pages need pagination — extracting a shared component prevents duplication.

## Files to Create
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/Pagination.svelte` — Shared pagination component
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/ui/index.ts` — Update barrel export

## Implementation Details

### Props
- `currentPage: number` — Current page (1-indexed)
- `totalPages: number` — Total number of pages
- `baseUrl?: string` — Base URL for page links (defaults to current page URL)
- `paramName?: string` — URL param name (default: 'page')
- `onPageChange?: (page: number) => void` — Optional callback (for client-side navigation)

### Rendering
- Previous button: disabled when currentPage === 1
- Next button: disabled when currentPage === totalPages
- Page indicator: 'Page X of Y' text centered between buttons
- Optional: page number buttons for first, last, and surrounding pages (1 ... 4 5 6 ... 20)
- All navigation uses SvelteKit goto() with updated URL search params
- Preserves existing URL params (search, filters, sort) when changing page

### URL-Based Navigation
- Default behavior: clicking Previous/Next updates ?page= param via href links
- Progressive enhancement: works without JS (standard links)
- With JS: uses goto() for SPA navigation
- Scrolls to top of content area after page change

### Styling
- Flex layout: [Previous] [Page X of Y] [Next]
- Button styles: use design tokens var(--color-*), var(--radius-md)
- Disabled state: reduced opacity, cursor-not-allowed
- Active page number highlighted with var(--color-primary-500)
- Responsive: buttons stack if needed on very small screens

### Accessibility
- nav element with aria-label='Pagination'
- Previous/Next buttons with aria-label='Go to previous page' / 'Go to next page'
- Current page announced: aria-current='page'
- Disabled buttons have aria-disabled='true'

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Previous/Next buttons navigate between pages
- [ ] Previous disabled on page 1, Next disabled on last page
- [ ] Page indicator shows 'Page X of Y'
- [ ] URL ?page= param updated on navigation
- [ ] Existing URL params preserved (filters, search, sort)
- [ ] Works without JavaScript (progressive enhancement)
- [ ] Scrolls to top after page change
- [ ] Accessible: proper ARIA labels and roles
- [ ] Uses design tokens for consistent styling
- [ ] Exported from ui barrel file

## Effort Estimate
2-3 hours

## Dependencies
- None (foundational UI component)
notes:
## Review Notes (Feb 2026)

### Code References to Use
- **Pattern consumers**: LIB-03, STUDIO-04, STUDIO-06 all need pagination
- **URL-based state**: Use SvelteKit `page.url.searchParams` for page/limit params
- **Library example**: `getUserLibrary({ page, limit })` already accepts pagination params
- **Existing Table**: Check if Table component already has pagination built-in before creating standalone

### Implementation Notes
- Component should accept: currentPage, totalPages, onPageChange
- Support URL-based navigation (preferred for SSR) and callback-based (for client-side)
- Accessible: proper aria labels, keyboard navigation

### No Backend Blockers
Pure UI component. Fully unblocked.

title: Document SVG cache invalidation strategy
description:
SVG files and raster images have different cache strategies but SVG path is hardcoded.

Locations: packages/image-processing/src/service.ts:79, 194

Current:
- Raster: public, max-age=31536000, immutable (1 year) - content-addressed keys
- SVG: public, max-age=31536000, immutable (same) - hardcoded logo.svg

Issue: SVG updates won't bust cache because filename doesn't change.

Options:
1. Add version hash to SVG filenames
2. Use shorter cache duration for SVG
3. Document the cache invalidation strategy

Decision needed on approach.
close_reason: SVG logos now use shorter cache (1 hour) instead of immutable. This allows logo updates to propagate without complex cache busting while still providing reasonable caching.

title: CONTENT-01: Content Detail Page Route
description:
## Content Detail Page Route

Create content detail page: `src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/content/[contentSlug]/+page.svelte` and `+page.server.ts`.

**Ref**: P1-FE-CONTENT-001, design/frontend/COMPONENTS.md, design/frontend/DATA.md

### Files to Create

1. `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/content/[contentSlug]/+page.server.ts`
2. `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/(space)/content/[contentSlug]/+page.svelte`

### +page.server.ts Implementation

Use `createServerApi()` from `$lib/server/api`. Import `ApiError` from `$lib/server/errors`. Import `ContentWithRelations` from `$lib/types`, `SingleItemResponse` from `@codex/shared-types`.

1. Extract `slug` and `contentSlug` from params
2. Create api: `const api = createServerApi(platform, cookies)`
3. Fetch content: `api.fetch('content', '/api/organizations/${slug}/content/by-slug/${contentSlug}')`
4. If `locals.user`, check access via `api.access.getStreamingUrl(content.id)` -- success=hasAccess, catch 403=no access
5. If hasAccess, fetch progress via `api.access.getProgress(content.id)` for resume position
6. For unauthenticated: `setHeaders({'Cache-Control': 'public, max-age=300, s-maxage=300, stale-while-revalidate=60'})`
7. Return `{ content, hasAccess, progress, streamUrl, previewUrl, organization: { slug } }`
8. Catch `ApiError` with status 404, throw `error(404, 'Content not found')`

### +page.svelte Implementation

Svelte 5 runes: `let { data }: { data: PageData } = $props()`

**SEO** in `<svelte:head>`: title, meta description (160 chars), og:title, og:description, og:image (thumbnailUrl), og:type=video.other

**Player section** (aspect-ratio 16/9, bg neutral-900, radius-lg):
- `hasAccess && streamUrl` -> player placeholder div (replaced by CONTENT-02)
- `previewUrl` exists -> preview placeholder div (replaced by CONTENT-03)
- else -> poster-only with thumbnail image

**Content details** (grid: 1fr + 320px sidebar, collapses at 768px):
- Main: h1 title, meta line (creator name, date, duration), description with h2
- Sidebar: creator card, purchase CTA placeholder when !hasAccess

### Access State Machine

| State | Player | Sidebar |
|---|---|---|
| Unauthenticated+free | Preview + "Sign in" | Sign in CTA |
| Unauthenticated+paid | Preview + "Sign in to purchase" | Sign in CTA |
| Authenticated+purchased | Full VideoPlayer + resume | Creator card |
| Authenticated+unpurchased | Preview + Purchase CTA | Purchase CTA |
| Creator/Admin | Full VideoPlayer | Creator card |

### Styling (CSS custom properties)

- `.content-page`: max-width 1200px, margin 0 auto, padding var(--space-4)
- `.player-section`: width 100%, aspect-ratio 16/9, bg var(--color-neutral-900), border-radius var(--radius-lg), overflow hidden
- `.content-details`: grid 1fr 320px, gap var(--space-6), single col at 768px
- `.sidebar`: flex column, gap var(--space-4)

### Existing Code Context

- Types: `ContentWithRelations` (extends Content with creator?, organization?, mediaItem?)
- Layout provides `data.org` (OrganizationData) and `data.user` from `+layout.server.ts`
- API: `createServerApi(platform, cookies)` with `.content`, `.access`, `.org`, `.checkout` + `.fetch()`
- Navigation config: `$lib/config/navigation.ts`

### Acceptance Criteria

- Route resolves at /{orgSlug}/content/{contentSlug}
- Server load fetches content + checks access
- SEO meta tags in svelte:head
- Content metadata: title, description, creator, duration, date
- Access state determines player placeholder
- Public metadata cached 300s stale-while-revalidate
- 404 for non-existent content
- Responsive grid collapses at 768px
notes:
## Review Notes (Feb 2026)

### Code References to Use
- **Remote function**: Use `getContentBySlug({orgSlug, contentSlug})` from `lib/remote/content.remote.ts` — NOT raw `api.fetch()`
- **Server API**: `api.content.list(params)` with slug filter, `api.access.getStreamingUrl(contentId)`
- **Types**: `ContentWithRelations` from `lib/types.ts`
- **Existing VideoPlayer**: `lib/components/VideoPlayer/VideoPlayer.svelte` — check completeness before building new

### API Corrections
- Access check pattern: Use `getStreamingUrl(contentId)` with try/catch on 403 — there is NO `/api/access/content/:id/check` endpoint
- If 403 → user doesn't have access → show purchase CTA
- If success → user has access → show player with streaming URL

### No Backend Blockers
All required endpoints exist. Content detail page is fully unblocked.

title: Phase 1.5: Seed Data & Global Templates
description:
## Overview Create seed script for global email templates. System won't work without these. ## Files to Create ### 1. Seed Script File: `packages/database/scripts/seed-email-templates.ts` ```typescript import { dbWs, schema } from '../src'; import { eq, and } from 'drizzle-orm'; const globalTemplates = [ { name: 'email-verification', scope: 'global' as const, subject: 'Verify your email address', htmlBody: \`

Verify your email

Hi {{userName}},

Please verify your email address by clicking the button below:

Verify Email

This link expires in {{expiryHours}} hours.

If you didn't create an account, you can safely ignore this email.

{{platformName}} - {{contactUrl}}

\`, textBody: \`Hi {{userName}}, Please verify your email address by visiting: {{verificationUrl}} This link expires in {{expiryHours}} hours. If you didn't create an account, you can safely ignore this email. {{platformName}}\`, status: 'active' as const, description: 'Sent when a new user registers to verify their email address', }, { name: 'password-reset', scope: 'global' as const, subject: 'Reset your password', htmlBody: \`

Reset your password

Hi {{userName}},

We received a request to reset your password. Click the button below:

Reset Password

This link expires in {{expiryHours}} hours.

If you didn't request this, you can safely ignore this email.

{{platformName}} - {{contactUrl}}

\`, textBody: \`Hi {{userName}}, We received a request to reset your password. Visit this link: {{resetUrl}} This link expires in {{expiryHours}} hours. If you didn't request this, you can safely ignore this email. {{platformName}}\`, status: 'active' as const, description: 'Sent when a user requests a password reset', }, { name: 'password-changed', scope: 'global' as const, subject: 'Your password has been changed', htmlBody: \`

Password Changed

Hi {{userName}},

Your password has been successfully changed.

If you didn't make this change, please contact support immediately:

{{supportUrl}}

{{platformName}}

\`, textBody: \`Hi {{userName}}, Your password has been successfully changed. If you didn't make this change, please contact support immediately: {{supportUrl}} {{platformName}}\`, status: 'active' as const, description: 'Sent when a user successfully changes their password', }, { name: 'purchase-receipt', scope: 'global' as const, subject: 'Receipt for your purchase', htmlBody: \`

Thank you for your purchase!

Hi {{userName}},

{{contentTitle}}

Amount: ${{priceFormatted}}

Date: {{purchaseDate}}

Watch Now

{{platformName}} - {{contactUrl}}

\`, textBody: \`Hi {{userName}}, Thank you for your purchase! {{contentTitle}} Amount: ${{priceFormatted}} Date: {{purchaseDate}} Watch now: {{contentUrl}} {{platformName}}\`, status: 'active' as const, description: 'Sent after a successful content purchase', }, ]; async function seedTemplates() { console.log('🌱 Seeding email templates...'); for (const template of globalTemplates) { const existing = await dbWs.query.emailTemplates.findFirst({ where: and( eq(schema.emailTemplates.name, template.name), eq(schema.emailTemplates.scope, 'global') ), }); if (existing) { console.log(\` ⏭️ Skipping \${template.name} (already exists)\`); continue; } await dbWs.insert(schema.emailTemplates).values({ ...template, organizationId: null, creatorId: null, }); console.log(\` ✅ Created \${template.name}\`); } console.log('✨ Seed complete!'); } seedTemplates() .then(() => process.exit(0)) .catch((err) => { console.error('Seed failed:', err); process.exit(1); }); ``` ### 2. Add npm script File: `packages/database/package.json` Add to scripts: ```json { "scripts": { "db:seed:templates": "tsx scripts/seed-email-templates.ts" } } ``` ### 3. Add to root package.json (optional) File: `package.json` ```json { "scripts": { "db:seed:templates": "pnpm --filter @codex/database db:seed:templates" } } ``` ## Template Token Reference ### Brand Tokens (from @codex/platform-settings) - `{{platformName}}` - Platform display name - `{{logoUrl}}` - Platform logo URL - `{{primaryColor}}` - Primary brand color (hex) - `{{secondaryColor}}` - Secondary brand color (hex) - `{{contactUrl}}` - Contact/support URL - `{{supportEmail}}` - Support email address ### Template-Specific Tokens - email-verification: `userName`, `verificationUrl`, `expiryHours` - password-reset: `userName`, `resetUrl`, `expiryHours` - password-changed: `userName`, `supportUrl` - purchase-receipt: `userName`, `contentTitle`, `priceFormatted`, `purchaseDate`, `contentUrl` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] Seed script created with all 4 templates - [ ] Script is idempotent (safe to run multiple times) - [ ] `pnpm db:seed:templates` works - [ ] Templates include both HTML and text versions - [ ] All placeholder tokens documented - [ ] Brand token placeholders match @codex/platform-settings

description:
Wire content-api to trigger transcoding after upload completion.

title: P1-TRANSCODE-001: Media Transcoding System
description:
Implement GPU-accelerated media transcoding pipeline with RunPod, HLS generation, and tiered storage.

**Architecture:**
- @codex/transcoding package (business logic)
- media-api worker (triggers + webhooks)
- RunPod Python worker (FFmpeg + audiowaveform)
- Tiered storage: R2 (delivery) + B2 (archival)

**Phases:**
- Phase 1: Codex-01i - Database Schema & Validation
- Phase 2: Codex-7tu - @codex/transcoding Package
- Phase 3: Codex-phu - media-api Worker
- Phase 4: Codex-7sp - RunPod Python Worker
- Phase 5: Codex-bjc - Content-API Integration
- Phase 6: Codex-fjd - Unit Tests
- Phase 7: Codex-ztf - Integration & E2E Tests
- Phase 8: Codex-dmb - CI/CD & Deployment

**Success Criteria:**
- Video → HLS ready in <10 min
- Audio → HLS + waveform ready in <2 min
- 95%+ success rate
- All tests passing
notes:

### ✅ Completed & Passing Tests

**media-api Worker Tests:**

title: Configure BetterAuth cross-subdomain cookies
description:
## Problem
Session cookies need to work across subdomains:
- `revelations.studio` (platform)
- `yoga-studio.revelations.studio` (org)
- `creators.revelations.studio` (creator hub)

## Solution
Add cross-subdomain cookie configuration to BetterAuth:

```typescript
// workers/auth/src/auth-config.ts
advanced: {
  crossSubDomainCookies: {
    enabled: true,
    domain: '.revelations.studio'
  }
}
```

## Verification Needed
1. Check if this config already exists in auth worker
2. Test cookie sharing across subdomains
3. Verify local dev still works (localhost doesn't use subdomain cookies)

## Impact
Without this, users would need to re-login when navigating between subdomains.

title: STUDIO-04: Studio Content Management
description:
## Objective
Build the Studio content management page with a DataTable listing all content for the organization. Includes title, type, status badges, view counts, revenue, and edit actions. Server-side pagination.

## Files
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/content/+page.svelte` — Content listing page
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/content/+page.server.ts` — Server load with pagination
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/ContentTable.svelte` — Content DataTable component

## Implementation Approach
1. **Server load** (`+page.server.ts`):
   - Read `page` and `limit` from URL search params (defaults: page=1, limit=20).
   - Fetch from `api.content.list({ organizationSlug, page, limit })`.
   - Return `{ content, pagination: { page, limit, total, totalPages } }`.

2. **ContentTable component**:
   - Use existing Table component pattern.
   - Columns:
     - **Title**: text, linked to content detail/edit
     - **Type**: badge (course / video / article)
     - **Status**: colored badge — green "Published", yellow "Draft"
     - **Views**: formatted number
     - **Revenue**: formatted currency (admin+ only column)
     - **Actions**: Edit button (link to `/studio/content/[id]/edit`)
   - Sortable column headers (client-side sort within current page).

3. **Page component**:
   - Header: "Content" title + "Create Content" primary button (links to `/studio/content/new`).
   - ContentTable with data from server load.
   - Pagination controls at bottom: Previous / Next buttons, "Page X of Y" display.
   - Empty state: "No content yet. Create your first piece of content!" with CTA button.

4. **Pagination**: URL-based pagination using `goto()` with updated search params. Preserves other params.

## API Endpoints
- `GET /api/content?organizationSlug=X&page=1&limit=20` → returns `{ items, pagination }`

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Status badges use appropriate colors (green=published, yellow=draft)
- [ ] "Create Content" button links to new content form
- [ ] Server-side pagination works with URL params
- [ ] Pagination controls show page X of Y with prev/next
- [ ] Revenue column visible only to admin+ roles
- [ ] Empty state renders when no content exists
- [ ] Edit action links to correct content edit page

## Effort Estimate
4–5 hours

## Backend Blocker: User-Facing Org Role Check

### Problem
STUDIO-01 (Studio Layout Shell) needs to check the current user's role in the org to show/hide admin features, but the membership endpoint is internal-only (HMAC).

### Options

2. Create a user-facing `GET /api/organizations/:orgId/my-membership` endpoint
3. Include role in org data when fetched by authenticated user

- Session data does not include org role information

title: Visual regression testing setup with Playwright
description: Set up visual regression testing for UI components. Configure Playwright visual comparison, establish baseline screenshots, test responsive breakpoints, theme variants (light/dark), and component states (hover, focus, disabled).
close_reason: Implemented Storybook interaction tests, visual regression testing, and test documentation

comments.author: brucemckay
comments.text:
SCOPE: Visual regression testing with Playwright screenshots

GOAL: Prevent UI regressions with automated visual comparison

SETUP:
- Use Playwright's toHaveScreenshot() matcher
- Store baseline screenshots in tests/__screenshots__/
- Run against Storybook stories (consistent state)
- Test multiple viewports and themes

WHAT TO TEST:
1. Component variants (Button: primary, secondary, outline, etc.)
2. Component states (hover, focus, disabled, loading, error)
3. Responsive breakpoints (mobile, tablet, desktop)
4. Theme variants (light mode, dark mode if implemented)
5. Layout components at different viewport sizes
6. Complex component states (open/closed, expanded/collapsed)

COMPONENTS TO SCREENSHOT:
Priority 1 (User-facing):
- Button (all variants + states)
- Input, TextArea (normal, error, disabled)
- Card, Badge
- Dialog (open state)
- Navigation components (Header, Tabs)

Priority 2 (Interactive):
- Accordion (collapsed/expanded)
- Dropdown (closed/open)
- Tooltip (visible state)
- Switch, Checkbox

TEST STRUCTURE:
Create: apps/web/tests/visual/components.visual.test.ts

Pattern per component:
1. Navigate to Storybook story
2. Wait for component to load
3. Take screenshot
4. Compare with baseline
5. Fail if diff > threshold (e.g., 0.2%)

VIEWPORTS TO TEST:
- Mobile: 375x667
- Tablet: 768x1024
- Desktop: 1280x720
- Wide: 1920x1080

THEME TESTING (if applicable):
- Set data-theme attribute
- Screenshot light mode
- Screenshot dark mode
- Compare both

CHALLENGES:
- Animations (disable or wait for completion)
- Fonts (ensure loaded)
- Dynamic content (use static test data)
- Flaky screenshots (consistent viewport, no hover states)

BASELINE GENERATION:
First run: pnpm test:visual --update-snapshots
CI runs: Compare against baseline, fail on diff

DELIVERABLE:
- Visual regression test suite
- Baseline screenshots committed
- CI integration
- Documentation on updating baselines

title: Library Continue Watching Section
description:
## Summary Add a "Continue Watching" section to the Library page that surfaces the 4 most recently watched in-progress items above the main content grid. This provides quick access to content the user has partially consumed. ## Context - **Current state**: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte` renders all library items in a flat grid. There is no separation of in-progress items. - **Design reference**: `design/roadmap/work-packets/frontend/P1-FE-ACCESS-001-library.md` includes a ContinueWatching component spec. - **Data available**: Library items already include `progress.percentComplete`, `progress.completed`, and `progress.updatedAt` fields from the access API. ## Requirements ### 1. ContinueWatching Component **File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/library/ContinueWatching.svelte` ```typescript interface Props { items: LibraryItem[]; } ``` - Filter items where `progress.percentComplete > 0 && !progress.completed` - Sort by `progress.lastWatched` (or `progress.updatedAt`) descending (most recent first) - Take first 4 items via `.slice(0, 4)` - Use `$derived` for the filtered/sorted list - **Conditionally render**: Only show section if `inProgress.length > 0` ### 2. Layout - Section header: `

Continue Watching

` styled with `font-size: var(--text-xl)`, `font-weight: var(--font-semibold)` - **Desktop** (min-width 768px): 4-column grid, `grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr)`, gap `var(--space-4)` - **Mobile** (below 768px): Horizontal scroll container - `display: flex; overflow-x: auto; scroll-snap-type: x mandatory; gap: var(--space-4); -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch` - Each card: `min-width: 260px; scroll-snap-align: start; flex-shrink: 0` - Hide scrollbar: `.continue-scroll::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none }` and `scrollbar-width: none` ### 3. Card Display - Reuse existing card pattern from the library page (thumbnail + progress bar + title) - Each card MUST show a progress bar at the bottom of the thumbnail with percentage - Progress bar: absolute positioned at bottom of thumbnail, full width, 4px height - Track: `var(--color-neutral-300)` - Fill: `var(--color-primary-500)`, width = `percentComplete%` - Show percentage text below title: `"{percent}% watched"` in `var(--text-xs)`, `var(--color-text-secondary)` ### 4. Integration into Library Page **Modify**: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte` - Import `ContinueWatching` component - Place `` ABOVE the main content grid - Add a visual separator (margin-bottom: `var(--space-8)`) between continue watching and main grid - The ContinueWatching section should NOT appear when the library is empty or loading ### 5. Data Flow - No additional API calls needed - filter from existing library data - The `libraryQuery.data` from TanStack already contains progress info - If a dedicated "recently watched" endpoint becomes available later, this can be swapped in ## Files to Create - `apps/web/src/lib/components/library/ContinueWatching.svelte` ## Files to Modify - `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte` ## Acceptance Criteria - [ ] ContinueWatching section appears above main grid when user has in-progress items - [ ] Shows max 4 items sorted by most recently watched - [ ] Does NOT appear when no items are in progress - [ ] Progress bar visible on each card thumbnail - [ ] Horizontal scroll on mobile with snap points - [ ] 4-column grid on desktop - [ ] Svelte 5 runes: `$props()`, `$derived()` used - [ ] Component only renders when `inProgress.length > 0` ## References - Design: `design/roadmap/work-packets/frontend/P1-FE-ACCESS-001-library.md` (Continue Watching section) - Existing page: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte` - Card pattern: existing `.content-card` styles in library page

title: Library Filtering & Search
description:
## Summary

Add filtering and search capabilities to the Library page (`/library`). Currently the library page renders a flat grid of all purchased content with no way to filter, search, or narrow results. This task adds three filter dropdowns (Organization, Content Type, Progress Status) and a debounced search input, all persisted in the URL for bookmarkability.

## Context

- **Current state**: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte` renders a content grid via `useLiveQuery` over `libraryCollection`. The server load (`+page.server.ts`) calls `api.access.getUserLibrary()` with no query params.
- **Design reference**: `design/roadmap/work-packets/frontend/P1-FE-ACCESS-001-library.md` specifies LibraryFilters and LibrarySearch components with exact interfaces.
- **API**: `api.access.getUserLibrary(params?: URLSearchParams)` already accepts URLSearchParams.

## Requirements

### 1. LibraryFilters Component
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/library/LibraryFilters.svelte`

```typescript
interface Props {
  orgs: { id: string; name: string; slug: string }[];
  filters: {
    org?: string;
    type?: 'video' | 'written';
    status?: 'not_started' | 'in_progress' | 'completed';
  };
  onchange: (filters: typeof filters) => void;
}
```

- Three `<Select>` dropdowns (from existing Melt UI Select component):
  - **Organization**: "All organizations" + user's orgs (fetched from org worker `/api/users/me/organizations`)
  - **Content Type**: "All types", "Video", "Written"
  - **Progress Status**: "All progress", "Not started", "In progress", "Completed"
- Each dropdown change calls `onchange` with merged filter state
- Wrap in `<div class="filters">` with flex layout, gap `var(--space-3)`, flex-wrap

### 2. LibrarySearch Component
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/library/LibrarySearch.svelte`

```typescript
interface Props {
  value?: string;
  onchange: (value: string) => void;
}
```

- Uses existing `Input` component with `type="search"` and search icon
- **Debounce**: 300ms delay before calling `onchange`
- Local `$state` for immediate input responsiveness
- `clearTimeout`/`setTimeout` pattern for debounce
- Min-width 200px, max-width 400px, flex: 1

### 3. URL-Based Filter State
**Modify**: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte`

- Read filters from `$page.url.searchParams` on load
- `handleFilterChange(newFilters)` builds `URLSearchParams` from non-empty values and calls `goto('/library?${params}', { replaceState: true })`
- Query param mapping: `?org=<slug>&type=<video|written>&status=<not_started|in_progress|completed>&q=<search>`
- **Reset page to 1** whenever any filter changes (add `page: 1` to filter change handler)

### 4. Server Load Updates
**Modify**: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.server.ts`

- Parse URL search params: `org`, `type`, `status`, `q`, `page`
- Build `URLSearchParams` from non-undefined values
- Pass to `api.access.getUserLibrary(params)`
- Also fetch user's organizations: `api.fetch('org', '/api/users/me/organizations', sessionCookie)` for the org filter dropdown
- Return `{ library, orgs, filters }` to page

### 5. Integration into Page
- Add `<LibrarySearch>` and `<LibraryFilters>` in a toolbar div above the content grid
- Toolbar: `display: flex; gap: var(--space-4); margin-bottom: var(--space-6); flex-wrap: wrap`
- On mobile (max-width 640px): `flex-direction: column`
- Show empty state with "Clear Filters" action when filters produce no results

## Files to Create
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/library/LibraryFilters.svelte`
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/library/LibrarySearch.svelte`

## Files to Modify
- `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte`
- `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.server.ts`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Three filter dropdowns render and update URL params on change
- [ ] Search input debounces at 300ms and updates `?q=` param
- [ ] Page reloads data when URL params change (SvelteKit invalidation)
- [ ] Filters reset to page 1 on change
- [ ] URL is bookmarkable - refreshing preserves filter state
- [ ] Empty state shows "Clear Filters" button when filters yield no results
- [ ] Mobile layout stacks toolbar vertically
- [ ] Svelte 5 runes (`$props`, `$state`, `$derived`) used throughout

## References
- Design: `design/roadmap/work-packets/frontend/P1-FE-ACCESS-001-library.md`
- Components: `design/frontend/COMPONENTS.md`
- Existing page: `apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/library/+page.svelte`
- API client: `apps/web/src/lib/server/api.ts` (see `access.getUserLibrary`)

title: AUTH-04: Account Layout and Protected Routes
description:
## Account Layout and Protected Routes Create the account section layout with sidebar navigation and auth guard that redirects unauthenticated users to login. ### Context - Design doc: design/frontend/AUTH.md — see Protected Routes section and Route Groups table - The account page at apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/+page.svelte exists as a skeletal profile page - Auth guard pattern from AUTH.md: check locals.user, redirect to /login?redirect=/account if missing ### Current State - apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/+page.svelte exists with a static profile form (no server wiring) - There is NO +layout.svelte or +layout.server.ts for the account section - No sidebar navigation exists - No auth protection exists ### Files to Create 1. CREATE: apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/+layout.server.ts (auth guard) 2. CREATE: apps/web/src/routes/(platform)/account/+layout.svelte (sidebar layout) ### Implementation Details #### +layout.server.ts (Auth Guard) import { redirect } from '@sveltejs/kit'; import type { LayoutServerLoad } from './$types'; export const load: LayoutServerLoad = async ({ locals, url }) => { if (!locals.user) { throw redirect(302, '/login?redirect=' + encodeURIComponent(url.pathname)); } return { user: locals.user, }; }; This ensures ALL routes under /account/* require authentication. The redirect param preserves the original URL so the user returns after login. #### +layout.svelte (Sidebar Navigation) Create a layout component with: 1. A sidebar with navigation links: - Profile (/account) — default, active by default - Notifications (/account/notifications) - Payment (/account/payment) 2. A main content area with for child pages 3. Active state highlighting based on current path 4. Responsive: sidebar collapses on mobile (stack vertically) 5. Use design tokens for all styling (var(--color-*), var(--space-*), etc.) Pattern:

Account Settings

{#each navItems as item} {item.label} {/each}
{@render children()}
Style with: - Sidebar: fixed width ~240px on desktop, full width stacked on mobile - Active link: highlight with var(--color-primary-500) background or left border - Use scoped

title: DNS & Subdomain Routing for Organizations
description:
Enable wildcard subdomain routing for organization storefronts.

**Goal:** When org `yoga-studio` is created, `yoga-studio.revelations.studio` should work automatically.

## Technical Approach

**Cloudflare Workers Routes** (NOT Custom Domains - wildcards not supported there)

### Required Configuration:

1. **DNS Records** (in Cloudflare dashboard):
   ```
   *    AAAA  100::  (proxied) - catches all subdomains
   @    AAAA  100::  (proxied) - root domain
   ```

2. **Worker Routes** (in wrangler.toml or dashboard):
   ```toml
   routes = [
     { pattern = "*.revelations.studio/*", zone_name = "revelations.studio" },
     { pattern = "revelations.studio/*", zone_name = "revelations.studio" }
   ]
   ```

3. **SvelteKit hooks.server.ts**: Extract subdomain and resolve org

### Why Routes not Custom Domains:
- Custom Domains don't support wildcards
- Routes support `*.domain.com/*` patterns
- Need DNS + Route together

### Future: Custom Domains (Phase 2)
For customer vanity domains like `learn.yogastudio.com`:
- Cloudflare for SaaS / Workers for Platforms
- Token-based auth redirect flow
- See ARCHITECTURE.md for auth strategy

**References:**
- https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/routing/routes/
- https://community.cloudflare.com/t/how-to-add-a-wildcard-domain-to-a-worker/525974
close_reason: All code implemented: wildcard routes, SvelteKit hooks, cross-subdomain cookies, org slug resolution. Remaining DNS record + e2e testing are deployment/ops tasks.

title: Add Cache-Control headers to all image R2 uploads
description:
## Problem
ImageProcessingService uploads images to R2 without Cache-Control headers:
```typescript
this.r2Service.put(keys.sm, variants.sm, undefined, {
  contentType: 'image/webp',
})
```

Since these images are served via R2 public custom domain with Cloudflare CDN, they need proper cache headers for edge caching to work.

## Solution
Add `cacheControl: 'public, max-age=31536000, immutable'` (1 year) to all image uploads. This matches the pattern in `branding-settings-service.ts:167`:
```typescript
await this.r2.put(r2Path, buffer, undefined, {
  contentType: mimeType,
  cacheControl: 'public, max-age=31536000',
});
```

Images are effectively immutable — when a user uploads a new avatar/thumbnail, it gets a new R2 key (different userId/contentId path), so aggressive caching is safe.

## Files to Modify
- `packages/image-processing/src/service.ts` — all `r2Service.put()` calls (6 total across 3 methods + SVG)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All R2 put() calls include cacheControl header
- [ ] WebP variants: `{ contentType: 'image/webp', cacheControl: 'public, max-age=31536000, immutable' }`
- [ ] SVG uploads: `{ contentType: 'image/svg+xml', cacheControl: 'public, max-age=31536000, immutable' }`
close_reason: Verified complete: SVG sanitization (line 189), R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE config (12 bindings), no S3 URLs in production, 10 cache headers, delete methods use key helpers, all tests passing

title: Phase 4: Memory profiling, quality checks, and stability testing
description:
## Objective
Complete Phase 4 verification tasks to ensure image processing is production-ready.

## Tasks
### Memory Profiling
- [ ] Test 5MB image processing doesn't cause OOM in Workers
- [ ] Profile Wasm memory usage during `processImageVariants()`
- [ ] Verify proper cleanup of `SafePhotonImage` (.free() calls)
- [ ] Test concurrent requests (10+ simultaneous uploads)

### Quality Checks
- [ ] Verify WebP output quality is acceptable
- [ ] Test resize quality: sm (200px), md (400px), lg (800px)
- [ ] Compare file sizes: WebP vs original JPEG/PNG
- [ ] Test edge cases: very small images, very large images, unusual aspect ratios

### Stability Testing
- [ ] 100+ consecutive uploads without memory leaks
- [ ] Test all supported formats: PNG, JPEG, WebP, GIF, SVG
- [ ] Test invalid inputs: corrupt images, wrong MIME types, oversized files
- [ ] Verify error handling gracefully fails

### Performance Testing
- [ ] Measure end-to-end latency for typical image upload
- [ ] Benchmark parallel variant generation (sm/md/lg)
- [ ] Verify R2 upload parallelization provides expected speedup

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] No OOM errors with 5MB images
- [ ] WebP quality >= 85% SSIM compared to original
- [ ] 100+ successful uploads without memory leaks
- [ ] All formats handled correctly
- [ ] Error responses are clear and actionable
- [ ] Performance baseline documented

**NOTE**: Must develop detailed test plan with specific metrics and thresholds. Track progress in task notes.
close_reason: Added stability.test.ts with 10 tests covering: memory safety patterns, variant configuration, error handling, format validation. Documented what requires deployed environment (OOM testing, quality benchmarks, load tests).

title: Refactor delete methods to use key helper functions instead of URL parsing
description:
## Problem
deleteUserAvatar() and deleteOrgLogo() reverse-engineer R2 keys from stored URLs by splitting on \`.s3.amazonaws.com/\`:

```typescript
// service.ts:271
const urlParts = user.image.split('.s3.amazonaws.com/');
const objectKey = urlParts[1];
const basePath = objectKey.replace(/lg\.webp$/, '');
```

This is fragile for three reasons:
1. The S3 URL format is wrong (should be R2 public URL)
2. When URLs change to R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE, all parsing breaks
3. The key helper functions (getUserAvatarKey, getOrgLogoKey, getContentThumbnailKey) already exist in @codex/transcoding

## Solution
**Option A (Preferred)**: Use the same key helper functions for both upload and delete:
```typescript
async deleteUserAvatar(userId: string): Promise<void> {
  const keys = ['sm', 'md', 'lg'].map(size => getUserAvatarKey(userId, size));
  await Promise.all(keys.map(key => this.r2Service.delete(key)));
  await this.db.update(schema.users).set({ avatarUrl: null }).where(eq(schema.users.id, userId));
}
```

**Option B**: Store the R2 key (not URL) in a separate DB column. More work, less immediate need.

## Files to Modify
- `packages/image-processing/src/service.ts` — deleteUserAvatar(), deleteOrgLogo()
- deleteOrgLogo needs to handle SVG vs raster (check logoUrl extension or store format in DB)

## Additional Notes
- deleteContentThumbnail() already uses getContentThumbnailKey() correctly — good pattern to follow
- For deleteOrgLogo SVG detection: use the key helper or store a \`logoFormat\` field
- Consider storing \`r2Key\` alongside \`url\` in the DB for future-proofing (like branding_settings stores logoR2Path)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] deleteUserAvatar uses getUserAvatarKey() helper
- [ ] deleteOrgLogo uses getOrgLogoKey() helper
- [ ] No URL string parsing in delete methods
- [ ] SVG vs raster detection doesn't rely on URL parsing
- [ ] All delete tests pass
close_reason: Verified complete: SVG sanitization (line 189), R2_PUBLIC_URL_BASE config (12 bindings), no S3 URLs in production, 10 cache headers, delete methods use key helpers, all tests passing

title: ECOM-02: Checkout Session & Redirect
description:
## Objective
Wire the PurchaseButton to create a Stripe Checkout session via the Ecom API and redirect the user to Stripe's hosted checkout page. Includes error handling for common failure cases.

## Files
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/commerce/PurchaseButton.svelte` — Update with checkout logic
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/content/[contentId]/+page.svelte` — Wire PurchaseCTA on content page
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/content/[contentId]/+page.server.ts` — Add form action for checkout

## Implementation Approach
1. **Form action approach** (`+page.server.ts`):
   - Add a `checkout` form action.
   - Receives `contentId` from form data.
   - Constructs `successUrl` and `cancelUrl` based on current org slug:
     - `successUrl`: `/purchase/success?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}`
     - `cancelUrl`: `/purchase/cancel`
   - Calls `api.checkout.create({ contentId, successUrl, cancelUrl })`.
   - Returns `{ url: stripeCheckoutUrl }` on success.
   - Returns `{ error }` on failure.

2. **PurchaseButton integration**:
   - Use SvelteKit's `enhance` for the form action.
   - On form submission:
     - Set loading state ("Redirecting to checkout...").
     - On success: `window.location.href = result.url` to redirect to Stripe.
     - On error: display error message, reset loading state.

3. **Error handling**:
   - **Network error**: "Unable to connect. Please check your internet connection and try again."
   - **Invalid content (404)**: "This content is no longer available."
   - **Already purchased (409)**: "You already own this content. Go to your library to access it." + link to library.
   - **Auth required (401)**: Redirect to login with return URL.
   - **Server error (500)**: "Something went wrong. Please try again later."

4. **Alternative: Remote Function approach** (if using TanStack):
   - Instead of form action, use a Remote Function `checkout.create(contentId)`.
   - Same error handling applies.
   - Choose based on what's available in the current data layer setup.

## API Endpoints
- `POST /api/checkout/create` → body: `{ contentId, successUrl, cancelUrl }` → returns `{ url }`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Clicking PurchaseButton creates a Stripe Checkout session
- [ ] User is redirected to Stripe's hosted checkout page
- [ ] Loading state shows during session creation
- [ ] Network errors display user-friendly message
- [ ] Already-purchased (409) shows message + library link
- [ ] Unauthenticated users redirected to login with return URL
- [ ] Invalid content shows appropriate error
- [ ] Success/cancel URLs are correctly constructed with org context

## Effort Estimate
2–3 hours

## Dependencies
- ECOM-01 (Purchase Components)
notes:
## Review Notes (Feb 2026)

### API Corrections
- Use `createCheckout` form from `lib/remote/checkout.remote.ts` — NOT raw form action
- Or use `createCheckoutSession` command for SPA-style redirect
- Both are already implemented with proper Stripe session creation and redirect

### Code References to Use
- **Form pattern**: `createCheckout` uses `form()` with `checkoutFormSchema` — progressive enhancement, works without JS
- **Command pattern**: `createCheckoutSession` uses `command()` — returns sessionUrl for client-side redirect
- **Toast**: Use `toast.error()` for checkout failures

### Implementation Notes
- `createCheckout` automatically redirects to Stripe (server-side 303)
- Success URL defaults to `/library?purchase=success`
- Cancel URL defaults to `/content/{contentId}`

### No Backend Blockers
Checkout endpoint exists. Remote functions already built.

title: Implement Cloudflare cache purge on content publish/unpublish
description:
## Summary
When content is published or unpublished, purge relevant Cloudflare edge cache to ensure immediate visibility.

## Trigger Events
| Event | URLs to Purge |
|-------|---------------|
| Content published | Content detail page, org explore page |
| Content unpublished | Content detail page, org explore page |
| Content edited (title/desc/thumb) | Content detail page |
| Org settings changed | Org landing page |

## Implementation

### Cloudflare API Call
```typescript
// packages/cloudflare-clients/src/cache-purge.ts
export async function purgeUrls(urls: string[], env: Bindings): Promise<void> {
  await fetch(
    \`https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/zones/\${env.CF_ZONE_ID}/purge_cache\`,
    {
      method: 'POST',
      headers: {
        'Authorization': \`Bearer \${env.CF_API_TOKEN}\`,
        'Content-Type': 'application/json'
      },
      body: JSON.stringify({ files: urls })
    }
  );
}
```

### Integration Points
1. **ContentService.publish()** → purge content + explore
2. **ContentService.unpublish()** → purge content + explore
3. **ContentService.update()** → purge content page (if public fields changed)

### URL Generation
```typescript
function getContentUrls(content: Content, org: Organization): string[] {
  const base = \`https://\${org.slug}.revelations.studio\`;
  return [
    \`\${base}/content/\${content.slug}\`,
    \`\${base}/explore\`
  ];
}
```

## Environment Variables
- `CF_ZONE_ID`: Cloudflare zone identifier
- `CF_API_TOKEN`: API token with Cache Purge permission

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Publish triggers cache purge
- [ ] Unpublish triggers cache purge (critical for takedowns)
- [ ] Purge is fire-and-forget (don't block on response)
- [ ] Errors logged but don't fail the main operation
- [ ] Test with curl to verify fresh content after purge
notes: Review fix: setCachePurge() now requires webAppUrl param (removed hardcoded domain). Callers must pass env.WEB_APP_URL.
close_reason: Closed

title: WP-13: Image Optimization
description:
## Objective
Implement image optimization for static and dynamic images across the platform.

## Reference Design Docs
- `design/frontend/COMPONENTS.md` - Image optimization section, ContentCard thumbnail handling, ResponsiveImage component, skeleton placeholders
- `design/frontend/FRONTEND_SPEC.md` - Frontend architecture and tooling

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `@sveltejs/enhanced-img` configured for static images
- [ ] Dynamic thumbnails served with responsive sizes
- [ ] Lazy loading for below-fold images
- [ ] Blur placeholder or skeleton during load
- [ ] AVIF/WebP format support
- [ ] Proper `sizes` attribute for responsive images
- [ ] Cloudflare Image Resizing configured (or fallback)

## Vite Config Change
Add `enhancedImages()` plugin to `apps/web/vite.config.ts`:
```typescript
// apps/web/vite.config.ts
import { enhancedImages } from '@sveltejs/enhanced-img';
import { sveltekit } from '@sveltejs/kit/vite';

export default {
  plugins: [
    enhancedImages(),
    sveltekit()
  ]
};
```

## Static Images (enhanced-img)

### Usage
```svelte
<!-- Automatically generates AVIF/WebP + srcset -->
<enhanced:img 
  src="./hero.jpg" 
  alt="Hero image"
  sizes="(max-width: 768px) 100vw, 50vw"
/>
```

Use `<enhanced:img>` for all static/build-time images: hero images, logos, marketing graphics, etc.

## ResponsiveImage Component
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/components/ResponsiveImage.svelte`

Props:
- `src` - Image source URL
- `alt` - Alt text (required)
- `sizes` - Responsive sizes attribute
- `widths` - Array of widths, default `[400, 800, 1200]`
- `loading` - Loading strategy, default `'lazy'`
- `class` - CSS class passthrough

Generates `srcset` from `getOptimizedImageUrl()` utility. Falls back to the middle width as the default `src`.

## Image Utility
**File**: `apps/web/src/lib/utils/image.ts`

### `getOptimizedImageUrl(url, width, format)`
- Uses Cloudflare Image Resizing when available
- Falls back to pre-generated size if not on Cloudflare CDN

### `getThumbnailUrl(contentId, size: 'sm'|'md'|'lg')`
- Maps to pre-generated thumbnail URLs from R2
- Thumbnail URL pattern: `{R2_PUBLIC_URL}/thumbnails/{contentId}-{size}.webp`

## ContentCard Thumbnail Integration
Use ResponsiveImage inside ContentCard with responsive sizes for grid layout:
`sizes="(max-width: 640px) 100vw, (max-width: 1024px) 50vw, 33vw"`

## Skeleton Placeholder CSS
- aspect-ratio: 16/9
- Background shimmer animation (linear-gradient sliding)
- Opacity transition on image load (0 -> 1 over 0.3s)
- Respects prefers-reduced-motion (disables shimmer animation)

## Key Files
- `apps/web/vite.config.ts` - Add enhancedImages() plugin
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/ResponsiveImage.svelte` - CREATE
- `apps/web/src/lib/utils/image.ts` - CREATE (getOptimizedImageUrl, getThumbnailUrl)

## Dependencies
- Codex-9ij (Platform Routes) - CLOSED
- Codex-zjq (Thumbnail Generation) - CLOSED
- Multi-size WebP thumbnails generated during transcoding (sm: 200px, md: 400px, lg: 800px)
- **This task is UNBLOCKED and ready to work.**

## Effort
Small (S) - ~3-5 hours
notes:
## Implementation Notes (Added 2026-02-11)

### All Dependencies Met
- Codex-9ij (Platform Routes) ✅ CLOSED
- Codex-zjq (Thumbnail Generation) ✅ CLOSED

### Implementation Plan
1. Configure @sveltejs/enhanced-img in vite.config.ts for static images
2. Create ResponsiveImage.svelte component for dynamic thumbnails from R2
3. Use pre-generated sizes from transcoding: {contentId}-{size}.webp
4. Add lazy loading (loading="lazy") to all below-fold images
5. Skeleton placeholder with shimmer during load
6. Proper sizes attribute for responsive srcset

title: Plan unified observability strategy (frontend + backend)
description:
## Summary
Design a cohesive observability strategy that spans frontend and backend.

## Planning Document
See `design/frontend/OBSERVABILITY_PLAN.md` for full planning context.

## Key Decisions Needed
1. **Error tracking tool**: Sentry cloud vs self-hosted vs alternative
2. **Log aggregation**: Where do frontend/backend logs go?
3. **Request correlation**: How does requestId flow end-to-end?
4. **Web Vitals**: Where do performance metrics go?
5. **Cost model**: What's acceptable spend?

## Deliverables
- [ ] Audit existing `@codex/observability` package
- [ ] Choose error tracking solution
- [ ] Design request ID propagation
- [ ] Define SLOs (uptime, latency targets)
- [ ] Update INFRASTRUCTURE.md with final decisions
- [ ] Create implementation issues

## Dependencies
Should be done before major frontend implementation begins.

This consolidates the full-stack approach covering: (1) Distributed Tracing via OpenTelemetry-compatible headers + correlation IDs, (2) Centralized Logging with Cloudflare Tail + structured JSON, (3) Error Reporting with Cloudflare Analytics Engine (Sentry future path documented), (4) Metrics via Workers Analytics + Analytics Engine for Web Vitals. Strategy aligns with Cloudflare-native approach for zero-cost Phase 1 while documenting clear upgrade paths.

title: Add reserved subdomain validation to org slug creation
description:
Organization slugs were not validated against the reserved subdomains list (cdn, auth, api, etc.) during creation or update. This meant someone could create an org with slug 'cdn' or 'auth', potentially conflicting with infrastructure subdomains.

Fixed by:
1. Added .refine() to organizationSlugSchema in @codex/validation that rejects reserved subdomains
2. Updated OrganizationService.isSlugAvailable() to return false for reserved slugs
3. Consolidated RESERVED_SUBDOMAINS to single source of truth in @codex/constants
4. Added RESERVED_SUBDOMAINS_SET export for O(1) lookups
5. Updated web app to import from @codex/constants instead of maintaining duplicate list
6. Added 3 missing entries (creators, platform, localhost) to the canonical list
7. Updated tests to use non-reserved slugs and added reserved slug rejection tests

Files changed:
- packages/constants/src/urls.ts
- packages/validation/src/content/content-schemas.ts
- packages/validation/src/__tests__/organization-schemas.test.ts
- packages/organization/src/services/organization-service.ts
- apps/web/src/lib/constants.ts
close_reason: Implemented reserved subdomain validation at Zod schema level and OrganizationService.isSlugAvailable(). Consolidated RESERVED_SUBDOMAINS to single source of truth in @codex/constants. All tests passing (317 validation + 40 organization + 15 web app).

title: Task 1: Project Initialization
description:
Initialize SvelteKit project with Cloudflare adapter.

## OBJECTIVE
Create the apps/web directory and initialize SvelteKit with correct dependencies.

## DOCUMENTATION
- Work Packet: design/roadmap/work-packets/frontend/P1-FE-FOUNDATION-001-project-setup.md#project-initialization
- SvelteKit Docs: https://kit.svelte.dev/docs/creating-a-project

## STEPS
1. Create apps/web directory.

title: SETTINGS-01: Branding Settings (Logo + Colors)
description:
## Objective
Build the Studio branding settings page with logo upload (preview, size/type validation) and primary + accent color pickers with presets, hex input, and native color input.

## Files
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/branding/+page.svelte` — Branding settings page
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/settings/branding/+page.server.ts` — Server load + form actions
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/ColorPicker.svelte` — Color picker with presets + hex input
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/LogoUpload.svelte` — Logo upload with preview + validation

## Implementation Approach
1. **Server load** (`+page.server.ts`):
   - Fetch current branding settings from org data (logo URL, primary color, accent color).
   - Return `{ branding: { logoUrl, primaryColor, accentColor } }`.

2. **LogoUpload component**:
   - Props: `currentLogoUrl`, `onUpload` callback.
   - Drag-and-drop zone or click-to-select file input.
   - Validation: max 2MB, image types only (image/png, image/jpeg, image/svg+xml, image/webp).
   - Preview: 80x80px circular preview of selected/current logo.
   - Client-side validation with error messages ("File too large", "Invalid file type").
   - On file select: show preview immediately, store file for form submission.

3. **ColorPicker component**:
   - Props: `label`, `value` (hex string), `onChange` callback.
   - Three input methods:
     a. **Native color input**: `<input type="color">` for visual picking.
     b. **6 preset buttons**: terracotta (#e85a3f), blue (#3b82f6), green (#10b981), purple (#8b5cf6), pink (#ec4899), amber (#f59e0b). Each is a small colored circle, selected state has ring.
     c. **Hex input**: text input with `#` prefix, validates 6-char hex format.
   - All three stay in sync — changing one updates the others.

4. **Page layout**:
   - Sections: "Logo" (LogoUpload) + "Brand Colors" (two ColorPickers: Primary, Accent).
   - Single "Save Changes" button at bottom.
   - Form action POSTs to server, which calls `api.org.updateSettings(orgId, { logo, primaryColor, accentColor })`.
   - Success/error toast feedback.
   - Backend handles KV cache invalidation for brand colors.

5. **Form action** (`+page.server.ts`):
   - Handle multipart form data (logo file + color values).
   - Upload logo to media service if changed.
   - Update org settings via API.
   - Return success/failure.

## API Endpoints
- `GET /api/org/:slug/settings` → returns current branding settings
- `PUT /api/org/:orgId/settings` → updates branding (logo, colors)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Logo upload with drag-and-drop and click-to-select
- [ ] Logo validation: max 2MB, image types only, with error messages
- [ ] Logo preview at 80x80px with current/selected image
- [ ] Primary and Accent color pickers with all three input methods
- [ ] 6 preset color buttons with visual selection state
- [ ] Hex input validates format and stays in sync with other inputs
- [ ] Save button submits all changes via form action
- [ ] Success/error toast feedback after save
- [ ] Brand colors update reflected after page reload (KV cache updated by backend)

## Effort Estimate
4–5 hours

## Dependencies
- STUDIO-01 (Studio Layout Shell)
notes:
## Review Notes (Feb 2026)

### API Corrections
- **Logo upload**: `POST /api/organizations/:id/settings/branding/logo` (multipart form)
- **Color update**: `PUT /api/organizations/:id/settings/branding` with `{ primaryColorHex }`
- **Note**: Only `primaryColorHex` is supported — no accent color field exists yet. Task should use single color picker, not dual.

### Code References to Use
- **Server API**: `api.org.getSettings(id)` for reading, individual PUT endpoints for updates
- **Toast**: `toast.success('Branding updated')` from `lib/components/ui/Toast`
- **Color picker**: Need to find/create color input component
- **File upload**: Reference media upload pattern from STUDIO-08

### No Backend Blockers
Branding endpoints exist. Simplify to single primary color (no accent).

title: Write Section 15: Security
description:
Write the Security section of FRONTEND_SPEC.md covering:

## Content:
- Content Security Policy (CSP) configuration
- XSS prevention (input sanitization, output encoding)
- CSRF protection (SvelteKit built-in)
- Cookie security (HttpOnly, Secure, SameSite)
- Input validation (client + server)
- Sensitive data handling (never log PII)

## Context7 Research:
- Query: 'SvelteKit security CSP CSRF cookies headers'
- Library: /sveltejs/kit
- Query: 'Content Security Policy headers'
- Library: /cloudflare/cloudflare-docs

## Security Headers:
- Content-Security-Policy: Restrict script/style sources
- X-Frame-Options: DENY (prevent clickjacking)
- X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
- Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
- Strict-Transport-Security: HTTPS enforcement

## Cookie Security (Auth Worker sets these):
- HttpOnly: true (no JS access)
- Secure: true (HTTPS only)
- SameSite: lax (cross-subdomain + CSRF protection)
- Domain: .revelations.studio (cross-subdomain)

## Input Validation:
- Client: Immediate feedback, UX only
- Server: ALWAYS validate, use Zod schemas
- Never trust client input

## Sources to consolidate:
- _archive/REMOTE_FUNCTIONS_AND_AUTH.md (security mentions)
- @codex/security package patterns

## Acceptance:
- CSP policy documented
- Security headers listed
- Cookie configuration specified
- Validation requirements defined
- PII handling guidelines established
close_reason: Batch writing sections 13-17

title: Optimize thumbnail compression for minimal storage cost
description:
## Context
Thumbnails are pre-generated during transcoding (Codex-zjq). This task ensures optimal compression settings.

## Requirements
- **WebP quality**: Find optimal quality setting (target 80-85 for good visual/size tradeoff)
- **JPEG fallback**: Generate for older browsers at similar quality
- **Dimensions**: Exact pixel dimensions for sm(200), md(400), lg(800)
- **File size targets**:
  - sm: <15KB
  - md: <40KB
  - lg: <100KB

## FFmpeg Settings to Test
```bash
# Extract frame and generate WebP
ffmpeg -i input.mp4 -vf 'select=eq(n\,0),scale=400:-1' -vframes 1 -c:v libwebp -quality 82 thumb-md.webp
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Benchmark different quality settings (75, 80, 82, 85)
- [ ] Document final FFmpeg command in RunPod worker
- [ ] Verify file sizes meet targets
- [ ] Visual quality spot-check on sample content
close_reason: Compression settings implemented in RunPod worker (sm=75, md=80, lg=82 WebP quality). File size targets documented in thumbnailVariants. JPEG fallback not needed given modern WebP support across browsers.

}

title: P1-NOTIFY-001: Email Notification Service
description:
## Overview
Build a DB-backed, provider-agnostic email notification system with global, organization, and creator template scopes.

## Key Deliverables
- Database schema for email templates with three scopes (global/org/creator)
- Template CRUD endpoints in notifications-api worker
- Template resolution with org and creator access rules
- Rendering with brand tokens from @codex/platform-settings
- Provider abstraction (Resend production, Console/MailHog dev)
- Core templates: email-verification, password-reset, password-changed, purchase-receipt

## Architecture
```
notifications-api Worker
    ↓
@codex/notifications (service package)
    ├── NotificationService (orchestration)
    ├── TemplateRepository (DB access)
    ├── TemplateRenderer (token replacement)
    └── Providers (Resend, Console, MailHog)
    ↓
@codex/platform-settings (branding tokens)
    ↓
@codex/database (email_templates table)
```

## Reference Implementation Plan
design/roadmap/implementation-plans/P1-NOTIFY-001-implementation-plan.md

## Definition of Done
- [ ] Templates stored in DB with global, org, creator scopes
- [ ] Access rules enforce org membership and creator ownership
- [ ] Template resolution with clear fallback order
- [ ] API endpoints with proper role checks
- [ ] Brand defaults from platform settings
- [ ] PII-safe logging
- [ ] Tests passing
- [ ] Documentation complete
