title: STUDIO-03: Studio Activity Feed
description:
## Objective
Build an activity feed component for the Studio dashboard showing the 10 most recent events (purchases, content publishes, customer signups) with icons, descriptions, and relative timestamps.

## Files
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/ActivityFeed.svelte` — Activity feed component
- `apps/web/src/lib/components/studio/ActivityItem.svelte` — Individual activity item
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/+page.svelte` — Integrate into dashboard (below stats)
- `apps/web/src/routes/_org/[slug]/studio/+page.server.ts` — Add activity fetch to server load

## Implementation Approach
1. **Server load** (update `+page.server.ts`):
   - Fetch activity from `api.admin.getActivity(orgId, { limit: 10 })`.
   - Return alongside existing stats data.

2. **ActivityItem component**:
   - Props: `type` (purchase | publish | signup), `description`, `timestamp`, `metadata`.
   - Icon per type: shopping cart (purchase), document (publish), user (signup).
   - Description text: e.g., "John D. purchased Advanced Course", "New Course published", "Jane S. signed up".
   - Relative timestamp using helper function: "2 hours ago", "yesterday", "3 days ago".

3. **ActivityFeed component**:
   - Vertical list with dividers between items.
   - "Recent Activity" heading.
   - Empty state: "No recent activity" message.
   - Scrollable if content overflows (max-height with overflow-y: auto).

4. **Relative time helper**: Create a `formatRelativeTime(date: Date): string` utility function. Use `Intl.RelativeTimeFormat` or manual calculation for "X minutes/hours/days ago".

5. **Integration**: Place ActivityFeed below the stat cards grid on the dashboard page.

## API Endpoints
- `GET /api/admin/:orgId/activity?limit=10` → returns `{ items: [{ type, description, timestamp, metadata }] }`

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Activity feed shows up to 10 most recent events
- [ ] Each event has an appropriate icon based on type
- [ ] Timestamps display as relative time ("2 hours ago", "yesterday")
- [ ] Empty state rendered when no activity exists
- [ ] Feed is scrollable when content overflows
- [ ] Integrates cleanly below stat cards on dashboard

## Effort Estimate
2–3 hours

## Dependencies
- STUDIO-01 (Studio Layout Shell)

title: Add thumbnail generation to transcoding pipeline
description:
## Problem
No image optimization for content thumbnails. Frontend needs responsive images for performance.

## Recommended Solution: Pre-generate during upload
Integrate thumbnail generation into existing RunPod transcoding pipeline:
- Generate 3 sizes: sm (200px), md (400px), lg (800px)
- Output formats: WebP (primary), JPEG (fallback)
- Store in R2 alongside transcoded media

## Why This Approach
- **Cheapest**: R2 storage is $0.015/GB/month, no per-request cost
- **Scalable**: Thumbnails served directly from R2/CDN, no processing at view time
- **Synergy**: Leverages existing RunPod pipeline, minimal new infrastructure
- **Predictable**: Fixed cost per upload, not per view

## Alternative Considered
Cloudflare Image Resizing ($0.50/1000 transformations) - good fallback but per-request cost adds up for popular content.

## Implementation
1. Update RunPod worker to extract frame and generate thumbnails
2. Add thumbnail URLs to content/media response
3. Frontend uses srcset for responsive loading

## Scope
- Modify transcoding worker (FFmpeg already available)
- Add thumbnail fields to content schema
- Update content-api to return thumbnail URLs
close_reason: Thumbnail generation implemented - multi-size WebP thumbnails added to transcoding pipeline.

title: Phase 7: Integration & E2E Tests
description:
Integration tests for media-api worker and E2E flows.

**Integration test files:**
- workers/media-api/src/__tests__/webhook.test.ts
- workers/media-api/src/__tests__/transcoding.test.ts

**Webhook endpoint tests:**
- Valid signature → 200, media updated
- Invalid signature → 401
- Missing signature → 401
- Malformed payload → 400

**Internal trigger tests:**
- Signed request → triggers job
- Unsigned request → 401
- Invalid media ID → 404

**E2E scenarios:**
1. Video Upload → HLS Ready
   - Upload → status=uploaded → trigger → status=transcoding → webhook success → status=ready

2. Audio Upload → HLS + Waveform Ready
   - Same flow, verify waveformKey set

3. Failed with Retry
   - Trigger → webhook failure → status=failed → retry → status=transcoding → success

4. Retry Limit Enforced
   - Failed with attempts=1 → retry → ValidationError

**Acceptance:**
- All E2E scenarios pass
- Webhook signature verification solid
- No flaky tests

**Depends on:** Phase 5 (full integration exists)
**Estimate:** 4-5 hours

Implemented Integration Tests for media-api:
- Endpoints verified: Health Check, Webhook (HMAC), Internal Trigger (Secret), Retry logic.

title: ACCT-01: Account Profile Save
P1-BE-CACHE-001: Branding Edge Cache
description:
Agent-executable epic for Edge Caching via Cloudflare KV.

## OBJECTIVE
Implement zero-latency branding cache using Cloudflare KV to eliminate FOUC.

## DOCUMENTATION
- Work Packet: design/roadmap/work-packets/backend/P1-BE-CACHE-001-edge-caching.md
- Cache Strategy: design/frontend/CACHE_STRATEGY.md

## ARCHITECTURE
Read-through caching at the edge:
1. Web SSR checks KV cache first (~10ms)
2. On miss, fetches from Organization API (~200ms)
3. Writes result to cache in background
4. Admin API invalidates cache on branding updates

## KEY DECISIONS
- Using wrangler.jsonc format (consistent with all workers)
- KV TTL: 7 days with instant invalidation on save
- Shared KV namespace between web app (read) and org-api (write)

## SUCCESS CRITERIA
- Cache hit latency <10ms
- No Flash of Unstyled Content
- Instant branding updates after Admin save
close_reason: All children completed

title: Task 1: KV Infrastructure Setup
description:
Create and bind the Cloudflare KV namespace.

## OBJECTIVE
Provision the BRAND_KV namespace and add bindings using JSONC format for consistency.

## DOCUMENTATION
- Work Packet: design/roadmap/work-packets/backend/P1-BE-CACHE-001-edge-caching.md#2-infrastructure
- Cache Strategy: design/frontend/CACHE_STRATEGY.md

## ARCHITECTURE NOTES
- Web app currently uses `wrangler.toml` but all workers use `wrangler.jsonc`
- This task includes converting web app config to JSONC for consistency
- KV namespace stores JSON-serialized brand tokens with 7-day TTL

## STEPS
1. Run: `wrangler kv:namespace create codex-brand-kv-prod`
2. Run: `wrangler kv:namespace create codex-brand-kv-preview --preview`
3. Copy both namespace IDs.
4. Convert `apps/web/wrangler.toml` → `apps/web/wrangler.jsonc`:
   - Use same structure as `workers/organization-api/wrangler.jsonc` as reference
   - Migrate all existing TOML config to JSONC
5. Add BRAND_KV binding to `apps/web/wrangler.jsonc`:
   ```jsonc
   "kv_namespaces": [
     {
       "binding": "BRAND_KV",
       "id": "<prod-id>",
       "preview_id": "<preview-id>"
     }
   ]
   ```
6. Add BRAND_KV binding to `workers/organization-api/wrangler.jsonc` (for cache invalidation).
7. Update `apps/web/src/app.d.ts`:
   ```typescript
   interface Platform {
     env: {
       // ... existing
       BRAND_KV?: KVNamespace;
     };
   }
   ```
8. Delete old `wrangler.toml` file.

## VALIDATION
- `wrangler dev` starts without BRAND_KV binding errors
- `platform.env.BRAND_KV` is accessible in server hooks
- JSONC config parses correctly (no syntax errors)

## PROGRESS
- [ ] Start
- [ ] Prod namespace created
- [ ] Preview namespace created
- [ ] wrangler.toml converted to wrangler.jsonc
- [ ] Web app BRAND_KV binding added
- [ ] Organization API BRAND_KV binding added
- [ ] app.d.ts types updated
- [ ] Old wrangler.toml deleted
- [ ] Complete

title: Task 4: Admin Save + Cache Invalidation
description:
Update Admin Settings to write to cache on save.

## OBJECTIVE
When Admin saves brand settings, update both Postgres AND KV for instant invalidation.

## DOCUMENTATION
- Work Packet: design/roadmap/work-packets/backend/P1-BE-CACHE-001-edge-caching.md#c-admin-api-integration
- Settings API: workers/organization-api/CLAUDE.md

## ARCHITECTURE
Write-on-Update pattern:
- Database is the source of truth
- KV cache is updated immediately after successful DB write
- Next request from ANY user worldwide receives new branding instantly

## AFFECTED ENDPOINTS
All three branding endpoints in `workers/organization-api/src/routes/settings.ts`:
1. `PUT /branding` - Update primary color
2. `POST /branding/logo` - Upload logo
3. `DELETE /branding/logo` - Remove logo

## STEPS
1. Add `BRAND_KV` binding to organization-api (done in Task 1).
2. Create cache update helper that constructs the `CachedBrandConfig` object:
   ```typescript
   function buildCacheObject(branding: BrandingSettingsResponse): CachedBrandConfig {
     return {
       version: Date.now(),
       updatedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
       branding
     };
   }
   ```
3. Update `PUT /branding` handler:
   - After successful DB update, get the org slug
   - Call `setBrandConfig(platform, slug, buildCacheObject(result))`
4. Update `POST /branding/logo` handler (same pattern).
5. Update `DELETE /branding/logo` handler (same pattern).
6. Consider adding "Clear All Brand Caches" admin utility (optional).

## VALIDATION
- Change color in Admin → Refresh frontend → New color appears immediately
- Verify KV value updated: `wrangler kv:key get brand:{slug} --namespace-id=<id>`
- No stale data on subsequent requests

## PROGRESS
- [ ] Start
- [ ] Cache update helper created
- [ ] PUT /branding cache invalidation added
- [ ] POST /branding/logo cache invalidation added
- [ ] DELETE /branding/logo cache invalidation added
- [ ] End-to-end test passed
- [ ] Complete

title: Task 5: Performance Verification
description:
Verify caching performance meets targets.

## OBJECTIVE
Confirm <10ms response for cached keys and no Flash of Unstyled Content (FOUC).

## DOCUMENTATION
- Work Packet: design/roadmap/work-packets/backend/P1-BE-CACHE-001-edge-caching.md#5-implementation-checklist
- Cache Strategy: design/frontend/CACHE_STRATEGY.md#why-this-is-better

## PERFORMANCE TARGETS
| Metric | Target | How to Measure |
|--------|--------|----------------|
| Cache hit latency | <10ms | CF Analytics / console.time |
| Cache hit rate | >99% | CF Analytics |
| FOUC | None | Visual inspection |
| First paint | Instant | Lighthouse |

## STEPS
1. Deploy to staging environment.
2. Warm the cache by loading an org page (first request populates cache).
3. Measure KV response time:
   - Option A: Cloudflare Workers Analytics → KV tab
   - Option B: Add `console.time('kv-read')` / `console.timeEnd('kv-read')` in layout
4. Load page multiple times, verify consistent <10ms.
5. Test cache invalidation:
   - Update branding in Admin
   - Refresh frontend
   - Verify new branding appears instantly (no stale data)
6. Test FOUC prevention:
   - Hard refresh page (Cmd+Shift+R)
   - Verify no flash of default styles before brand colors appear
7. Run Lighthouse audit for first paint metrics.

## VALIDATION
- Cache hit latency <10ms measured
- No FOUC on page load
- Cache invalidation works immediately
- Lighthouse first paint is acceptable

## PROGRESS
- [ ] Start
- [ ] Deployed to staging
- [ ] Cache warmed
- [ ] KV timing measured
- [ ] <10ms confirmed
- [ ] Cache invalidation tested
- [ ] FOUC prevention verified
- [ ] Lighthouse audit passed
- [ ] Complete

title: Task 2: Brand Cache Service
description:
Implement the brand-cache.ts service layer.

## OBJECTIVE
Create `src/lib/server/brand-cache.ts` with get/set/delete functions for KV caching.

## DOCUMENTATION
- Work Packet: design/roadmap/work-packets/backend/P1-BE-CACHE-001-edge-caching.md#b-the-cache-service
- Cache Strategy: design/frontend/CACHE_STRATEGY.md

## DATA SCHEMA
The cached value is a "Frontend-Ready" JSON object:
```typescript
interface CachedBrandConfig {
  version: number;
  updatedAt: string;  // ISO timestamp
  branding: {
    logoUrl: string | null;
    primaryColorHex: string;
  };
}
```

Key format: `brand:{org_slug}` (e.g., `brand:acme-corp`)

## STEPS
1. Create directory `apps/web/src/lib/server/` if it doesn't exist.
2. Create `brand-cache.ts` with:
   - Constants: `CACHE_KEY_PREFIX = 'brand:'`, `TTL_SECONDS = 604800` (7 days)
   - Type: `CachedBrandConfig` interface
   - Function: `getBrandConfig(platform, slug)` - Read from KV, return JSON or null
   - Function: `setBrandConfig(platform, slug, data)` - Non-blocking write using `waitUntil`
   - Function: `deleteBrandConfig(platform, slug)` - Remove entry (optional, for debugging)
3. Handle edge cases:
   - Return `null` if `platform?.env?.BRAND_KV` is undefined (local dev fallback)
   - Use try/catch for KV errors
4. Create `brand-cache.test.ts` with mocked KVNamespace:
   - Test cache hit returns data
   - Test cache miss returns null
   - Test KV unavailable returns null
   - Test setBrandConfig calls put with correct TTL

## VALIDATION
- TypeScript compiles without errors
- Unit tests pass for cache hit and miss scenarios
- No type errors when importing in layout

## PROGRESS
- [ ] Start
- [ ] Server directory created
- [ ] brand-cache.ts created
- [ ] CachedBrandConfig type defined
- [ ] getBrandConfig implemented
- [ ] setBrandConfig implemented
- [ ] deleteBrandConfig implemented
- [ ] Unit tests added
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] Complete
